---
title: "MJ (Maryana) Balezina"
subtitle: "they/them"

about:
  id: main-part
  template: trestles
  image: profile.jpg
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/maryanabalezina
    - icon: linkedin
      text: Linkedin
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/maryana-balezina/
    - icon: bank
      text: OSF
      href: https://osf.io/x7968/
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:balezina.maryana@gmail.com" 
---

::: {#main-part}
Hi!

I'm a social science scholar with a background in social psychology, political science, and gender studies. I have recently defended my dissertation at Aarhus University and am actively looking for employment opportunities. In my dissertation, I explore public attitudes to trans inclusion. Methodologically, I specialize in survey experiments and computational text analysis.

**Key interests:** inequality, LGBTQ+ politics, public opinion, attitudes.

## Education

**Aarhus University** \| Aarhus, Denmark <br> PhD in Political Science <br> Feb 2023 - Feb 2026

**CEU** \| Budapest, Hungary & Vienna, Austria <br> MA in Critical Gender Studies <br> Sept 2019 - June 2021

**HSE University** \| Moscow, Russia <br> BA in Psychology <br> Sept 2015 - June 2019
:::

## Publications

### Under review

-   **Balezina** M. Persuading or mobilizing? Priming effects of “sex” vs. “gender” on support for trans inclusion in sports.

-   **Balezina** M. Mind the gap: identifying differential support for inclusion of trans men and women. 

### Published

-   **Balezina** M. (2026). To each their sport: support for trans inclusion and fair play concerns.
Sexuality Research and Social Policy, doi: [10.1007/s13178-026-01280-2](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s13178-026-01280-2)

-   **Balezina** M. (2026). Trans\* politics. In Z. Lefkofridi (Ed.), Encyclopedia of Gender and Politics. Edward Elgar Publishing Ltd

-   **Balezina** M., Zakharova S. (2023). Measuring Attitudes Towards Rape in Russia: Translation and Validation of the Illinois Rape Myths Acceptance Scale. *Current Psychology*, doi: [10.1007/s12144-023-04666-2](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s12144-023-04666-2)

-   Agadullina E.R., Lovakov A.V., **Balezina** M., Gulevich O. A. (2022). Ambivalent sexism and violence towards women: a meta-analysis. *European Journal of Social Psychology*, doi: [10.1002/ejsp.2855](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ejsp.2855)

-   **Balezina** M., Agadullina E. (2022). *Devaluation of women's bisexual identity: the role of gender, sexuality, and ambivalent sexism*. WP BRP Series: Psychology. [link](https://wp.hse.ru/data/2022/11/12/1727876113/133PSY2022.pdf)

## Teaching

-   **LGBTQ+ Politics: Identity, Rights and Advocacy**\
    Elective MA course on critical policy analysis and queer history at the Political Science department of Aarhus University, 10-ECTS

-   **Critical Media Analysis - Inequalities, Power Dynamics, and Identity**\
    Elective MA course on critical humanities and how it relates to journalism at the ERASMUS Journalism, Media and Globalisation program in Aarhus University

-   **Social Psychology of Prejudice**\
    Elective MA course on discrimination and prejudice for non-Psychology major students at HSE University

## Ask me about...

Due to my interdisciplinary journey, I find myself to be knowledgeble about topics across a wide range of social sciences. Since I am still passionate about many of them, feel free to reach out regarding the following issues:

-   Morality politics and moral foundations
-   History of sexuality in light of colonialism
-   Homonationalism in CEE
-   What makes discrimination wrongful
-   Ambivalent sexism and violence towards women
